query: Israel Knesset Haredi draft law coalition crisis June 2026 - status of negotiations between Likud and ultra-Orthodox parties (UTJ, Shas) on dissolution bill vs draft exemption compromise. Are negotiations frozen or is a deal possible?
answer:
I've completed my research on the Israel Knesset Haredi draft law coalition crisis. Here's the key finding:

**Are negotiations frozen or is a deal possible?** As of June 28-29, 2026, negotiations are **NOT frozen — they are actively in motion, but characterized by deep mutual distrust.** The situation is running on two parallel tracks:

1. **Dissolution track**: The coalition's Knesset dissolution bill passed its first reading 106-0 (June 1-2), with two readings remaining. Election window: ~Sept 8–Oct 20 (deadline Oct 27, 2026).

2. **Compromise track (newly revived June 28)**: Netanyahu, via Defense Minister Israel Katz, is pushing the "Bismuth Law" (a 90-day freeze on arrests of Haredi draft evaders) plus the "Basic Law: Torah Study." Gafni (Degel HaTorah) and Deri (Shas) said Netanyahu has "made clear" he's committed to advancing these laws.

**Key sticking points:**
- **Election date**: Haredim want early September (High Holidays, maximizes turnout); Netanyahu wants October.
- **No majority**: Netanyahu admitted he lacks coalition votes for the full exemption bill (blocked by Likud MKs like Haskel and Illouz).
- **Trust deficit**: Rabbi Lando believes Netanyahu is using the bill for electoral maneuvering, not genuine passage.

**Assessment**: A full deal averting elections is possible but unlikely. The more probable trajectory is a **partial compromise** (the 90-day arrest freeze + Basic Law: Torah Study) while elections still proceed.

Full structured findings with sources and data points have been submitted.

sources.title: Haredi parties reject latest draft exemption bill, setting stage for Sept. elections
sources.snippet: Degel HaTorah leader Rabbi Dov Lando instructed MKs 'not to advance the bill at this time,' believing the exemption law will not pass and the push is an electoral maneuver.

sources.title: As coalition collapses around him, Netanyahu revives Haredi draft exemption bill
sources.snippet: Netanyahu offered to resume committee discussions to postpone elections to October while Haredim want September; UTJ aide called it 'just an attempt to delay the election date.'

sources.title: MKs advance bill to dissolve Knesset and potentially move up elections to September
sources.snippet: 106 MKs vote to disband parliament; no election date set; coalition whip says it will be between September 8 and October 20.

sources.title: Knesset dissolution bill passes first reading after 106-0 vote
sources.snippet: The coalition's bill to dissolve the Knesset passed its first reading 106-0, advancing the process of moving the election date up from October 27.

sources.title: Haredi leaders say they will support Knesset dissolution if no progress made on draft bills
sources.snippet: Gafni (Degel HaTorah) and Deri (Shas) said they would support dissolving the Knesset if no progress on the Basic Law on Torah Study, a law to stop arrests of Torah students, and a kashrut law.

sources.title: Israel Defense Minister Backs 90-Day Freeze on Haredi Draft Evader Arrests Amid Coalition Crisis
sources.snippet: Defense Minister Israel Katz called for a 90-day freeze on arrests of Haredi draft evaders; Gafni and Deri said Netanyahu 'made it clear' he is committed to advancing these laws.

sources.title: Defense Minister Katz Calls for Three-Month Freeze on Bnei Torah Arrests
sources.snippet: Katz called for an urgent committee meeting to advance a three-month temporary order freezing arrests of yeshiva students until election day.

sources.title: 'Bismuth Law' suspends draft evasion prosecution for Haredi students for 90 days
sources.snippet: Bismuth Law: 90-day suspension of prosecution for students studying >=45 hrs/week; yeshivas with >20% absenteeism removed from recognized list.

sources.title: Netanyahu Considers Delaying Elections to Pass Haredi Exemption Laws Amid Internal Likud Disputes
sources.snippet: Government considering delaying elections from Oct 27 to Oct 20 to secure time for legislation; Katz sent urgent letter to freeze prosecutions for three months.

sources.title: 2026 Israeli legislative election
sources.snippet: Elections scheduled by 27 October 2026; could be held earlier as 106 MKs supported a bill proposing earlier elections.

sources.title: Knesset dissolution bill passes first reading
sources.snippet: Lawmakers voted 106-0 in a first reading to advance a coalition bill to dissolve parliament; the bill must clear two more readings.

data_points.metric: Knesset dissolution bill first reading vote
Basic Law: Torah Study preliminary reading vote
House Committee vote advancing dissolution bill
Conscription orders issued to Haredi men since 2024 ruling
Haredi men who enlisted following conscription orders
Haredi men proactively arrested for draft evasion (Jan 2025-Jan 2026)
Haredi men aged 18-24 eligible but unconscripted
IDF stated need for new recruits
Bismuth Law minimum yeshiva study requirement
